package_footprint.python_package_dependency_model: The AW Python package currently depends on the bundled planning, memory, and verification module distributions unconditionally.
package_footprint.repo_footprint_rule: Presets choose checked-in repository footprint; routing-only minimizes installed repo state, not Python package dependencies.
package_footprint.why: Keeping first-party modules installed with the root command preserves simple installed-CLI lifecycle orchestration while generic module manifests and optional-module loading are still being shaped.
future_options: add optional extras for selected first-party modules
load module distributions lazily from generic module declarations
publish a separate core orchestrator and first-party bundle
must_preserve: routing-only as smallest checked-in repo footprint
planning, memory, and verification as first-party examples of the same module contract third parties use

participation_model.product_model: Agentic Workspace is a repo-native operating substrate for agents. It provides a recommended skill-guided operating loop, while repo config and module declarations can add obligations, tools, state, reports, gates, proof routes, lifecycle hooks, roots, and workflow phases without becoming separate first-contact concepts.

recommended_loop.purpose: Orient the agent through the thin startup adapter and compact routing commands before opening deeper state.
recommended_loop.module_extension_rule: Modules may contribute startup routing hints, prompts, reports, or gates, but startup must expose them through compact routing rather than broad raw reads.

recommended_loop.purpose: Decide direct, bounded, lane, or epic shape and preserve current intent, stop conditions, and continuation owner.
recommended_loop.module_extension_rule: Modules may add state owners, lifecycle hooks, or workflow phases when active work needs them; they must declare owned roots and proof or closeout effects.

recommended_loop.purpose: Load or capture only durable anti-rediscovery knowledge that is cheaper than re-deriving it.
recommended_loop.module_extension_rule: Modules may contribute resources or prompts for durable context, but must not blur active sequencing, issue history, or broad documentation into Memory.

recommended_loop.purpose: Select evidence for the changed surface and keep proof separate from semantic completion permission.
recommended_loop.module_extension_rule: Modules may contribute proof routes, verification protocols, evidence bundles, or gates; proof routes must name result schemas and safety metadata.

recommended_loop.purpose: Reconcile proof, intent satisfaction, residue, and next owner before any completion or issue-closure claim.
recommended_loop.module_extension_rule: Modules may add closeout obligations or residue routes, but parent/lane closure remains blocked until the owning intent and proof boundaries are satisfied.

task_posture_model.purpose: A compact, task-specific instruction packet that lets AW behave like a richer configurable AGENTS.md while keeping the static adapter small.
emitted_by: agentic-workspace summary when active planning owns continuation
inputs: changed paths and active planning state
task_posture_model.composition_rule: Workspace computes the packet from repo config, task facts, and selected module participation declarations. Optimization bias changes density and routing emphasis, artifact posture constrains generated or persisted outputs, initiative posture limits unsolicited work, assurance posture raises review/proof/delegation requirements, read budget gates raw context exposure, and workflow obligations add stage-specific requirements.
task_posture_model.module_rule: Modules may declare posture fragments, output requirements, review rubrics, authority boundaries, proof or closeout effects, and route triggers. Startup includes only fragments whose triggers match the task, changed paths, active state, or requested command.
must_not: grow AGENTS.md with every possible module instruction
emit all module posture fragments on ordinary startup
let optimization bias or output posture override hard workflow obligations
let module-provided posture silently change authority or proof ownership

workflow_obligation_model.role: Repo-configured obligations compose with the recommended loop by stage and scope tags. They are explicit, queryable, and reportable; they do not replace module ownership or make every obligation a new module.
workflow_obligation_model.composition_rule: Matched obligations raise the force of an existing loop step or closeout gate; unmatched obligations stay quiet.

conflict_model.rule: Conflicts are surfaced as report or lifecycle facts, not resolved silently.
conflict_model.resolution_owner: Agent chooses the safe route from compact evidence; human or repo owner decides policy conflicts that change accepted workflow.

projection_boundaries.source_of_truth: module_registry.json plus workspace config and module-owned manifests
must_not: make generated projections the source of module behavior
force all modules into fixed loop slots
promote first-party modules from examples into privileged special cases
increase ordinary startup burden by exposing all module detail up front

dynamic_instruction_projection.static_adapter_role: AGENTS.md remains an authority pointer and bootstrap adapter, not the place where every task posture, module rule, and workflow obligation is inlined.
dynamic_instruction_projection.projection_rule: Startup and changed-path commands emit a compact instruction/routing projection selected from task facts, repo config, active planning state, and module participation declarations.
selection_inputs: workflow obligation stage and scope tags
projection_outputs: selector and verbose routes for deeper detail
dynamic_instruction_projection.adherence_rule: The projection must carry provenance for repo config, workflow obligation, and module fragments so conflicts can be reported and closeout can say which posture applied.

posture_visibility_model.startup_visibility: Compact startup outputs show the active task posture only when it changes the allowed next action, proof burden, skill route, authority boundary, or read budget.
posture_visibility_model.report_visibility: Workspace reports expose applied workflow obligations, module contributions, posture conflicts, and selector routes without making every module report part of first contact.
posture_visibility_model.closeout_visibility: Closeout must reconcile proof, intent, and residue against the applied task posture when posture changed completion permission, output shape, review, or authority boundaries.

first_party_examples.example_role: active execution state, lane/slice decomposition, continuation owner, and honest closeout
durable anti-rediscovery knowledge and residue routing that reduces repeated context work
soft verification protocols, bounded evidence, proof route hints, and known gaps

component_model.rule: Module components are declarative projection metadata. A future adapter may project them to MCP resources, tools, prompts, schemas, and roots, but core package operation must not require an MCP runtime.

component_classes.semantics: read-only queryable context, state, or report output
executable operation, including mutating lifecycle work
reusable skill, handoff, or operating template
input, output, or structured-result contract referenced by resources and tools
owned path or boundary used to scope resources and tools

adapter_boundary.future_adapter: optional separate adapter package or wrapper
adapter_must_not: become the source of truth for module behavior
add FastMCP or MCP runtime dependencies to the core package
expose source-checkout-only maintainer tooling as host-repo components by default
blocking_prerequisites: schema or typed-validator coverage for referenced structured results
explicit safety metadata for mutating tools

scope.role: root orchestration components that compose selected modules
scope.rule: Root workspace components describe the shipped host-repo command surface; module-specific behavior stays in modules[*].components.

resources.description: Resolved workspace configuration, including local invocation posture when present.
Minimum safe startup context for ordinary first contact.
resources.source: startup context contracts and resolved config

resources.description: Compact current planning and continuation state.
Router-shaped workspace health, warnings, selectors, and module report gateway.
Changed-path proof selection and surface-value review guidance.

tools.name: workspace.init
tools.description: Initialize Agentic Workspace in a host repository.

tools.name: workspace.install
tools.description: Install selected workspace modules in a host repository.

tools.name: workspace.upgrade
tools.description: Refresh managed workspace and module surfaces.

tools.name: workspace.uninstall
tools.description: Remove managed workspace and selected module surfaces when ownership is clear.

prompts.name: workspace.startup
prompts.description: Ordinary startup route through AGENTS.md and compact context commands.

module_profiles.label: Routing only
module_profiles.activation: Use when the repo only needs compact entrypoint, config, and report routing surfaces.
module_profiles.cost_model: smallest checked-in repo footprint; no module state selected.
module_profiles.selection_rule: absence of selected modules plus root routing surfaces

module_profiles.label: Planning
module_profiles.activation: Use when active execution state, execplan routing, or lane closeout must be recoverable.
module_profiles.cost_model: adds checked-in planning state and active-work proof surfaces.
module_profiles.selection_rule: expands to the bundled planning module

module_profiles.label: Memory
module_profiles.activation: Use when durable repo knowledge should prevent repeated rediscovery.
module_profiles.cost_model: adds checked-in routed knowledge surfaces without active execution planning.
module_profiles.selection_rule: expands to the bundled memory module

module_profiles.label: Full
module_profiles.activation: Use when both active execution state and durable repo knowledge are worth the shared footprint.
module_profiles.cost_model: installer convenience profile; does not imply source-checkout maintainer tooling or optional advanced surfaces.
module_profiles.selection_rule: expands to every bundled module marked include_in_full_preset

feature_tiers.label: Routing only
feature_tiers.activation: Use when the repo only needs compact entrypoint, config, and report routing surfaces.
feature_tiers.cost_model: smallest shared footprint; no checked-in module state required.

feature_tiers.label: Planning
feature_tiers.activation: Use when active execution state, execplan routing, or lane closeout must be recoverable.
feature_tiers.cost_model: adds checked-in planning state and active-work proof surfaces.

feature_tiers.label: Memory
feature_tiers.activation: Use when durable repo knowledge should prevent repeated rediscovery.
feature_tiers.cost_model: adds checked-in routed knowledge surfaces without active execution planning.

feature_tiers.label: Full
feature_tiers.activation: Use when both active execution state and durable repo knowledge are worth the shared footprint.
feature_tiers.cost_model: current full preset; does not imply source-checkout maintainer tooling or optional advanced surfaces.

advanced_features.label: Review artifacts and promotion
advanced_features.activation: Enable when a host repo intentionally keeps review artifacts and promotion evidence.
advanced_features.default_surface_policy: Evidence/history only; not ordinary startup input.

advanced_features.label: External tracker adapters
advanced_features.activation: Enable when reconciling optional provider-agnostic evidence from a host tracker.
advanced_features.default_surface_policy: Optional adapter; core planning consumes only provider-agnostic evidence.

modules.name: planning
modules.description: Repo-native execution planning bootstrap and maintenance.
startup_steps: Read the active feature plan in `.agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/` when the TODO surface points there.
Check the roadmap in `state.toml` (authoritative) only when promoting work.

posture_triggers: task shape is broad, lane-shaped, or issue-linked
completion or archive claims are being made

dynamic_projection.startup_contribution: Expose active plan/TODO pressure, allowed next action, forbidden implementation paths, planning-required skills, and proof/closeout posture only when planning state or task shape requires it.
dynamic_projection.report_contribution: Report planning health, active state, continuation view, proof posture, closeout status, and stale or conflicting planning records.
dynamic_projection.closeout_contribution: Require proof-to-intent reconciliation, residue routing, parent/lane boundary checks, and explicit continuation owner before completion claims.

authority_boundaries: Planning may own active execution sequencing and closeout state.
Planning must not decide semantic issue completion without proof and acceptance reconciliation.
Planning must not become a general backlog or durable knowledge store.

resources.description: Current planning state, active items, queued items, and roadmap lanes.
Active and archived execution plans, opened only when compact state points there.

tools.name: planning.install
tools.description: Install planning bootstrap surfaces in a host repository.

prompts.name: planning.autopilot
prompts.description: Execute one bounded planning milestone from checked-in planning surfaces.

prompts.name: planning.reporting
prompts.description: Report active planning state and proof expectations from summary JSON.

modules.name: memory
modules.description: Durable repository knowledge bootstrap and maintenance.
startup_steps: Read `.agentic-workspace/memory/repo/index.md` only when memory is installed and the task is not already well-routed.
Read `.agentic-workspace/memory/WORKFLOW.md` only when changing memory behavior or the memory workflow itself.

root_agents_cleanup_blocks.block:
<!-- agentic-memory:workflow:start -->
Read `.agentic-workspace/memory/WORKFLOW.md` for shared workflow rules.
<!-- agentic-memory:workflow:end -->
root_agents_cleanup_blocks.label: memory workflow pointer block

posture_triggers: closeout residue contains reusable anti-rediscovery knowledge
startup has insufficient context after compact routing

dynamic_projection.startup_contribution: Expose memory consultation guidance and read-first durable notes only when task facts or changed paths justify them.
dynamic_projection.report_contribution: Report memory freshness, manifest routing, stale notes, and capture affordances without making notes part of ordinary startup.
dynamic_projection.closeout_contribution: Route durable lessons to Memory only when they are stable, reusable, and cheaper to retain than rediscover.

authority_boundaries: Memory may own durable anti-rediscovery knowledge and capture hygiene.
Memory must not own active execution state, issue history, proof authority, or general documentation.

resources.description: Memory repository manifest and routing metadata.
Freshness and routing health for durable knowledge notes.

tools.name: memory.install
tools.description: Install memory bootstrap surfaces in a host repository.

prompts.name: memory.router
prompts.description: Route to the narrowest relevant checked-in memory notes.

prompts.name: memory.capture
prompts.description: Capture durable repo knowledge into checked-in memory.

modules.name: verification
modules.description: Repo-native soft verification protocols, bounded evidence records, and proof route hints.
startup_steps: Use `agentic-workspace report --section verification --format json` only when the task, changed paths, assurance requirements, or proof selection activates verification protocols.
Use `agentic-verification report --target <repo> --format json` when working directly on the Verification module boundary.
sources_of_truth: Host repo protocols and bounded evidence: `.agentic-workspace/verification/manifest.toml`

posture_triggers: task or changed paths activate verification protocols
proof selection needs bounded evidence or known-gap reporting
closeout must distinguish evidence from completion permission

dynamic_projection.startup_contribution: Stay quiet unless task facts, changed paths, or assurance requirements activate verification protocols.
dynamic_projection.report_contribution: Expose configured protocols, proof route hints, evidence bundles, known gaps, and validation warnings.
dynamic_projection.closeout_contribution: Make proof evidence and known gaps visible while preserving Planning or human ownership of completion claims.

authority_boundaries: Verification may contribute proof protocols and bounded evidence.
Verification must not become generic proof authority, CI ownership, Planning state, or issue-close permission.

resources.description: Configured verification protocols, proof routes, evidence bundles, and known gaps.

tools.name: verification.report
tools.description: Inspect repo-native verification protocols and bounded evidence.
